summary: Broad match-progress PR that recorded 14 matched functions and 8 failed attempts, but the merged diff slice only shows small decomp-enabling cleanups across menu, game-mode, stage type, and item code. The concrete changes include refactoring mn_80230198 into a clearer switch over menu mode while preserving matching behavior, adding documented offsets and bitfields to grHomeRun_GroundVars, changing a gm_1601.h prototype parameter from s32 to int, and adding an explicit default case in itlugia.c. A later comment says this large PR was split into smaller subsystem draft PRs for review, with some commits dropped because equivalent or better decompilations were already upstream.
what_changed: In src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c, mn_80230198 was rewritten from goto-heavy range checks on arg2 into a switch over a renamed mode parameter. Modes 0, 2, and 4 perform frame lookup and call mn_8022ED6C; modes 1, 3, 5, and 6 do nothing.
In mnmainrule.c, AnimLoopSettings float literals in mn_803EC734 and mn_803EC770 were changed from lowercase f suffixes to uppercase F suffixes.
In src/melee/gr/types.h, grHomeRun_GroundVars gained offset comments, an explicit pad_D4[0x14], and a union exposing byte xE8 plus named one-bit fields xE8_b0 through xE8_b7.
In src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h, fn_80161C90's second parameter type changed from s32 to int.
In src/melee/it/items/itlugia.c, it_802D24A0's switch over item cases gained an explicit default: break before applying multiplier to ip->x40_vel.z.

The PR body listed many additional matched functions across groldkongo.c, ittincle.c, grhomerun.c, gm_1601.c, ifstatus.c, itmatadogas.c, itoldottosea.c, mnmainrule.c, and itzgshell.c, but those files/functions are not all present in this diff slice.
smart_moves: Converted brittle goto/range-test control flow in mn_80230198 into a compact switch that documents the exact active and no-op modes while keeping the same observable behavior for matching.
Renamed mn_80230198's arg2 to mode, improving local intent without overclaiming a higher-level semantic name.
Preserved the matching-sensitive PAD_STACK(8) in mn_80230198 while cleaning up variable types and loop form.

Added explicit layout documentation to grHomeRun_GroundVars with both relative struct offsets and ground-parameter offsets, making future stage decomp work less dependent on guessing raw xC4/xE8 fields.
Represented xE8 both as a raw byte and as bitfields, which lets future code match either bytewise operations or individual flag tests without losing layout clarity.
Added a default switch arm in itlugia.c rather than changing multiplier behavior, a minimal source-level adjustment likely aimed at matching compiler control flow.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function has tangled gotos that only encode a small set of accepted enum-like values, a switch with grouped cases can be both clearer and match-preserving.
Keep stack-padding macros such as PAD_STACK when simplifying source; they may still be required for stack layout even after control-flow cleanup.
For stage ground variables, explicit offset comments and padding fields help stabilize struct layout and make later field naming safer.
Use unions for unknown flag bytes when both whole-byte access and bit-level access may be needed by different functions.

Small prototype type changes, such as s32 to int, can matter for consistency with recovered code style or matching signatures, but this slice does not provide enough evidence to infer the exact compiler effect.
Large multi-subsystem match-progress PRs can become hard to review; splitting by subsystem was explicitly recommended/applied here.
naming_conventions: Function names remain address-based where semantics are not recovered, e.g. mn_80230198, fn_80161C90, fn_80162068, and it_802D24A0.
A generic parameter arg2 was renamed to mode in mn_80230198 when local control flow showed it selected behavior.
Unknown grHomeRun_GroundVars fields retain offset-style names such as xC4, xC6, xC8, xCC, xD0, and xE8.
Padding is named by offset as pad_D4.
Bitfields for an unknown byte are named xE8_b0 through xE8_b7, preserving both the byte offset and bit index.
Existing type names such as AnimLoopSettings, JObjIndices, MenuKindData, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, and Item_GObj are reused rather than replaced with speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Grouped switch cases in mn_80230198 replace an equivalent web of comparisons and gotos, reducing source complexity while maintaining which modes execute the frame-setting path.
Loop condition in mn_80230198 was normalized from i != 5 to i < 5.
The frame-selection loop checks mn_803EC770[i] first, then mn_803EC734[i], assigning settings to the matching range before calling mn_8022ED6C.
PAD_STACK(8) was retained in mn_80230198, indicating stack layout remained matching-sensitive.
Explicit default: break in itlugia.c can be used to influence switch codegen without changing the post-switch velocity scaling.
Uppercase float suffixes were used in static animation tables; the slice does not prove whether this was style-only or matching-relevant.
review_feedback: A PR comment says the large PR was split into four smaller draft PRs by subsystem for easier review: #2402 for ft/ fighters, #2404 for gr/ stages, #2405 for gm/ + mn/ game loop/menu, and #2406 for it/ items.
The same comment says commits for lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800244F4, grOldPupupu_8021119C, and fn_8023DAEC in mnnamenew.c were dropped because upstream already had equivalent or better decompilations.
No inline review comments or formal review records were present in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Inspect split PR #2405 for the full gm_1601 and mnmainrule matching work that came from this PR.
Inspect split PR #2404 for grHomeRun_GroundVars usage and any follow-up naming of xE8 flags.
Inspect split PR #2406 for the itlugia.c default-case tactic and other item matches.
Check whether mn_80230198's mode values have an enum or menu-rule semantic name elsewhere before renaming beyond mode.
Search for xE8_b0 through xE8_b7 references to determine which grHomeRun_GroundVars flags can be named semantically.
